briefdescription.para: SHA2 hash operation.
AES encryption operation.
AES decryption operation.
ECC signature generation operation.
RSA PSS signature generation operation.
RSA PSS signature verification operation.
Maximum number of operations.
Operation identifiers for tracking different crypto operations.
To notify the call back from client library.
Variable holds the status of the AES operation from client library and gets updated during callback.
SHA2 input data for hashing.
SHA2 hash output buffer.
AES plaintext data.
AES AAD data.
AES key.
AES IV.
AES-GCM expected CipherText.
AES-GCM expected Tag.
AES Tag output buffer.
AES Encrypted data output buffer.
AES Decrypted data output buffer.
ECC P256 private key for sign generation.
ECC hash/digest input for sign generation.
ECC signature output buffer.
RSA modulus (2048 bits)
RSA private exponent.
RSA public exponent.
RSA input data for signing.
RSA PSS signature output buffer.

memberdef.name: XAsu_OpContexts
memberdef.initializer: = { { .OpType = XASU_OP_SHA2_HASH, .Status = XST_FAILURE, .IsCompleted = FALSE, .OpName = "SHA2 Hash (LOW priority)" }, { .OpType = XASU_OP_AES_ENCRYPT, .Status = XST_FAILURE, .IsCompleted = FALSE, .OpName = "AES-GCM Encrypt (HIGH priority)" }, { .OpType = XASU_OP_AES_DECRYPT, .Status = XST_FAILURE, .IsCompleted = FALSE, .OpName = "AES-GCM Decrypt (HIGH priority)" }, { .OpType = XASU_OP_ECC_SIGN_GEN, .Status = XST_FAILURE, .IsCompleted = FALSE, .OpName = "ECC Sign Gen (HIGH priority)" }, { .OpType = XASU_OP_RSA_PSS_SIGN_GEN, .Status = XST_FAILURE, .IsCompleted = FALSE, .OpName = "RSA PSS Sign Gen (LOW priority)" }, { .OpType = XASU_OP_RSA_PSS_SIGN_VER, .Status = XST_FAILURE, .IsCompleted = FALSE, .OpName = "RSA PSS Sign Ver (HIGH priority)" } }

briefdescription.para: Array of callback contexts, one for each crypto operation.

detaileddescription.para: These contexts allow the callback function to identify which operation completed.

briefdescription.para: < X.509 certificate buffer

detaileddescription.para: Actual size of certificate

briefdescription.para: ECC signature buffer.
To notify the call back from client library.
Variable holds the status of the OCP operation from client library and gets updated during callback.

detaileddescription.para: Attestation data

briefdescription.para: To notify the call back from client library.
Variable holds the status of the OCP-UDE operation from client library and gets updated during callback.
Nonce buffer.
To notify the call back from client library.
Variable holds the status of the OCP-UDE operation from client library and gets updated during callback.
Encrypted UDE private key buffer for storing encrypted output.
This function performs AES encryption and decryption operation on a given data using AES-GCM engine mode.

simplesect.para:
Verify the additional status if operation flag is set to XASU_FINISH.
XASU_AES_TAG_READ, if encryption operation successfully done.
XASU_AES_TAG_MATCHED, if decryption operation successfully done.
Any other value shall be treated as failure.

inbodydescription.para: Verify additional status as well if operation flag set to XASU_FINISH to know operation is successful.
Verify additional status as well if operation flag set to XASU_FINISH to know operation is successful.

briefdescription.para: This function performs AES encryption and decryption operation on a given data using AES-CTR engine mode.
This function prints the data from given array on the console.

parameterdescription.para: Length of the data to be printed on console

briefdescription.para: Call back function which will be registered with library to notify the completion of request.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the call back reference.

Status of the request will be passed as an argument during callback
0 Upon success
Error code from ASUFW application upon any error

briefdescription.para: This main function to call the XAsu_AesExample function to perform AES encryption and decryption operation on given payload data.

detaileddescription.para: This main function to perform UDE private keys encryption and updates to efuses.
This main function to call the OCP example functions to perform DevAk attestation and generate the X.509 certificate for device keys.
Main function to demonstrate multi-crypto queue operations.

listitem.para: Upon successful encryption and decryption operation, it returns XASUFW_SUCCESS.
Error code on failure.
XST_SUCCESS if all operations complete successfully.
XST_FAILURE if any operation fails.
XST_SUCCESS, if example is run successfully.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure.

inbodydescription.para: Wait for device identity and attestation key generation.
Generate X.509 DevIk CSR(Certificate Signing Request).

briefdescription.para: Executes the multi-crypto example demonstrating concurrent queue operations.

detaileddescription.para:
This function demonstrates how to:
Send multiple crypto commands simultaneously with different priorities
Use unique callback contexts to track which operation completed
Wait for all operations to complete
Verify results from each operation

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if all operations complete successfully.
XST_FAILURE if any operation fails.

briefdescription.para: Sends crypto requests Set 1 and waits for completion.

detaileddescription.para:
Set 1 operations:
SHA2 hash with LOW priority
AES encryption with HIGH priority
ECC signature generation with HIGH priority
RSA PSS signature generation with LOW priority

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if all operations complete successfully.
XST_FAILURE if any operation fails.

briefdescription.para: Sends crypto requests Set 2 and waits for completion.

detaileddescription.para:
Set 2 operations (dependent on Set 1 results):
AES decryption with HIGH priority (uses encryption output from Set 1)
RSA PSS signature verification with HIGH priority (uses signature from Set 1)

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if all operations complete successfully.
XST_FAILURE if any operation fails.

briefdescription.para: Unified callback function for all crypto operations.

detaileddescription.para: This callback function demonstrates how to identify which operation completed when multiple operations are pending. The CallBackRef points to the unique XAsu_CallbackContext structure for each operation.

NOTE: Each operation passes its own context structure as CallBackRefPtr. When the callback is triggered, we cast CallBackRef back to XAsu_CallbackContext to identify which specific operation completed and update its status.

briefdescription.para: Prints and verifies the results of all crypto operations.
Prints data array with a label.

parameterdescription.para: Label string to print before data

briefdescription.para: Callback function which is registered with library to get request completion request.

parameterdescription.para: Status of the request is passed as an argument during callback.

briefdescription.para: This function sends request to ASUFW to get the DevIk X.509 certificate.

para.verbatim: The purpose of this is to illustrate how to use ASUFW client API for DevIk certificate generation.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if DevIk certificate is retrieved successfully.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: This function sends request to ASUFW to get the DevAk X.509 certificate.

para.verbatim: The purpose of this is to illustrate how to use ASUFW client API for DevAk certificate generation.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if DevAk certificate is retrieved successfully.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: This function sends request to ASUFW to get the DevIk CSR.

para.verbatim: The purpose of this is to illustrate how to use ASUFW client API for DevIk CSR generation.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if CSR is retrieved successfully.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: This function sends attestation request to ASUFW sign the data using device attestation key.

detaileddescription.para: The purpose of this is to illustrate how to use ASU client API for data attestation.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if attestation is run successfully.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the data from given array on the console.

parameterdescription.para: Length of the data to be printed on console.

briefdescription.para: Callback function which is registered with library to get request completion request.

parameterdescription.para: Status of the request is passed as an argument during callback.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the data from given array on the console.

parameterdescription.para: Length of the data to be printed on console.

briefdescription.para: Callback function which is registered with library to get request completion request.

parameterdescription.para: Status of the request is passed as an argument during callback.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the data from given array on the console.

parameterdescription.para: Length of the data to be printed on console.

briefdescription.para: This function encrypts the data using UDE KEK in ASUFW.

parameterdescription.para: Length of data to be encrypted in bytes
Pointer to buffer where encrypted data will be stored

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - if encryption was successful
XST_FAILURE - On failure of AES Encrypt Initialization, AES Encrypt data.

memberdef.argsstring: (XAsu_ClientParams *ClientParamPtr, XNvm_ClientInstance *NvmClientInstancePtr, u8 EncFlag, u8 PrgmFlag, u8 UdeKeyId)
memberdef.name: XAsu_OcpUdeEncNdPrgmUdeKey

briefdescription.para: This function encrypts the UDE private keys and program them to efuses.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_ClientParams structure which holds the client input parameters.
Flag to indicate whether to encrypt the key.
Flag to indicate whether to program the key.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successfully programming encrypted UDE private keys
XST_FAILURE - On Failure.

briefdescription.para: AES payload data length in bytes.
AES AAD length in bytes.
AES Key length in bytes.
AES Iv length in bytes.
AES Tag length in bytes.
Disable data cache for shared memory access.
Wait for Event - efficient low-power wait for interrupt/event.

detaileddescription.para: Uses architecture-specific wait instruction to put CPU in low-power state until an event occurs.

briefdescription.para: AES payload data length in bytes.
AES Key length in bytes.
AES IV length in bytes.
AES Tag length in bytes.
AES AAD length in bytes.
SHA2 hash length in bytes.
SHA2 input data length.
ECC P256 key size in bytes.
Double ECC P256 size in bytes.
RSA 2048 key size in bytes.
RSA PSS salt length in bytes.
RSA input data length in bytes.
Set 1: SHA2, AES Encrypt, ECC Sign, RSA Sign.
X.509 certificate buffer size.
Attestation data size.
ECC signature length.
Value Two.
Value Eight.
This example illustrates the usage of ASU AES client APIs for AES GCM and CTR engine modes.

detaileddescription.para: This example illustrates the usage of ASU OCP-UDE challenge request client APIs.
This example illustrates the usage of ASU OCP client APIs.
This example demonstrates how to use the ASU's queueing mechanism to send multiple cryptographic commands with different priorities and different crypto functions.

Encrypt the input data with provided key and IV, the output result is then decrypted back to get original data and verifies the GCM tag. The test fails, if decryption does not produce the original data or mismatch in GCM tag.

para.heading: Procedure to link and compile the example for the default ddr less designs

detaileddescription.para: The default linker settings places a software stack, heap and data in DDR memory. For this example to work, any data shared between client running on A78/R52/PL and server running on ASU, should be placed in area which is accessible to both client and server.
To keep things simple, by default the cache is disabled in this example using XASU_ENABLE_CACHE macro.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 am 07/10/24 Initial release am 08/25/24 Added XASU_DISABLE_CACHE macro am 09/24/24 Added SDT support

detaileddescription.para:
It illustrates:
Sending multiple concurrent commands to the ASU using different priorities
Using different crypto functions (AES, SHA2, RSA, and ECC/ECDSA)
Tracking which response corresponds to which request using unique callback contexts
Proper handling of the ASU queue scheduler with mixed priority operations

The example sends multiple crypto operations simultaneously:
SHA2 hash operation (LOW priority)
AES encryption operation (HIGH priority)
ECC sign generation operation (HIGH priority)
RSA PSS sign generation operation (LOW priority)
AES decryption operation (HIGH priority) - depends on encryption result
RSA PSS sign verification operation (HIGH priority) - depends on sign generation result

Each operation uses a unique callback context structure to identify which operation completed when the callback is triggered. This demonstrates the proper way to handle multiple concurrent requests in the ASU architecture.

para.heading: Procedure to link and compile the example for the default ddr less designs

detaileddescription.para: The default linker settings places a software stack, heap and data in DDR memory. For this example to work, any data shared between client running on A78/R52/PL and server running on ASU, should be placed in area which is accessible to both client and server.
To keep things simple, by default the cache is disabled in this example using XASU_DISABLE_CACHE macro.
NOTE: This example is for demonstration purposes only. The test data and keys used here are not intended for production use.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 kp 01/28/26 Initial release - Multi-crypto queue example

para.heading: Procedure to link and compile the example for the default ddr less designs

detaileddescription.para: The default linker settings places a software stack, heap and data in DDR memory. For this example to work, any data shared between client running on A78/R52/PL and server running on ASU, should be placed in area which is accessible to both client and server.
To keep things simple, by default the cache is disabled in this example using XASU_ENABLE_CACHE macro.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 rmv 08/04/25 Initial release rmv 09/08/25 Fixed missing initialization of SignatureBufLen in OcpDevAkAttestParam

para.heading: Procedure to link and compile the example for the default ddr less designs

detaileddescription.para: The default linker settings places a software stack, heap and data in DDR memory. For this example to work, any data shared between client running on A78/R52/PL and server running on ASU, should be placed in area which is accessible to both client and server.
To keep things simple, by default the cache is disabled in this example using XASU_ENABLE_CACHE macro.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 yog 08/20/25 Initial release
